Compute a predictive performance metric

evalMetric.Rd

Evaluate the predictive performance of a vector of predictions on observed outcomes under a specified criterion. Used internally by plot.eta and plot.box.

Usage

evalMetric(pred, y, criteria)

Arguments

pred

A numeric vector of predicted values on the response scale. For binomial criteria these should be probabilities in \([0, 1]\); for Poisson, non-negative rates.

y

A numeric vector of observed outcomes (same length as pred).

criteria

A string specifying the criterion. One of:

  • "gaussian.mspe": mean squared prediction error.

  • "gaussian.rsq": coefficient of determination.

  • "binomial.dev": binomial deviance.

  • "binomial.mcfrsq": McFadden's R-squared.

  • "binomial.tjursq": Tjur's R-squared (mean prediction gap between positives and negatives).

  • "binomial.auc": area under the ROC curve.

  • "poisson.dev": Poisson deviance.

Value

A numeric scalar: the value of the criterion.

See also

plot.eta, BRIERi.selection

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
 set.seed(1)
 y <- rnorm(100)
 pred <- y + rnorm(100, sd = 0.5)
 evalMetric(pred, y, "gaussian.mspe")
 evalMetric(pred, y, "gaussian.rsq")
} # }
